name: Spektral
tagline: AI-powered real-time image segmentation — green screen without the green screen
description: Danish computer vision startup acquired by Apple for $30M in 2017. Its real-time background segmentation technology was integrated into Portrait mode and ARKit.
story: Originally called CloudCutout, Spektral was a Copenhagen-based startup that developed AI-powered real-time image segmentation — the ability to separate foreground from background in video without a green screen. The technology worked on-device, processing video frames in real time using deep learning. Apple acquired Spektral in December 2017 for approximately $30 million, though the deal wasn't disclosed until October 2018. Spektral's technology was integrated into Apple's Portrait mode, the Clips app, and ARKit's augmented reality features. The co-founder became a manager of computational imaging at Apple, and Spektral as an independent company ceased to exist — another Nordic AI team absorbed into Apple's vast machine learning operation.
